workflow_name: Executive Meeting Transcript -> Manager Brief + Asks
user_message: Can you work with me on migration architecture for Evergreen Solutions?

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Evergreen Solutions
Date: 2024-01-22
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions), David Chen (Microsoft), Priya (Evergreen Solutions)

[00:02:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:06:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Priya (Evergreen Solutions): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:12:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:15:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

handoffs.condition: business review narrative input from exec meeting

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF50-I trap (Skip-level follow-up generic); produces complete Executive brief (strategic density), asks section (actionable for exec), risk register, skip-level f
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF50-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF50-I failure mode (Skip-level follow-up generic)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF50-I -- Skip-level follow-up generic
